revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳 |名称=岡古墳 |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市藤井寺４丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|34|N|135|35|37|E|scale:2000...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=岡古墳 |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市藤井寺４丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|34|N|135|35|37|E|scale:20000}} |形状=方墳 |規模=全長33m |築造年代=４世紀後半 |出土品=銅鏡 船形埴輪 |特記事項=住宅建設により、ほぼ消滅 }} '''岡古墳'''（岡古墳）とは、大阪府藤井寺市藤井寺4丁目731－1に所在した[[方墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]初期の古墳であることが発掘調査で判明したが、住宅建設によりほぼ消滅している。
== 概要== 岡古墳は 天皇陵に指定されている[[岡ミサンザイ古墳]]の周濠東側に位置する方墳であり、同古墳の[[陪塚]]ではないかと見られていた。住宅建設の予定があり、1980年（昭和55年）に大阪府教育委員会、1981年（昭和56年）には藤井寺市教育会によって[[発掘調査]]がなされた。調査によると、この古墳は2段に築造されており基底部で長さ34，5ｍ、高さ5ｍを測る。墳丘からは黒斑のある[[円筒埴輪]]のほか家型埴輪、船形埴輪が出土している。内部構造は割竹形[[木棺]]を蔵した粘土槨の２分の１が遺存していた。[[粘土槨]]残存部は 長さ2，５ｍ、幅１，8ｍであった。木棺の本体はすでに腐朽していたが、粘土槨に印された痕跡により、おおよその形状と大きさを知ることができ、外径80ｃｍの割竹形木棺であることが明らかとなった。棺内からは３面の[[青銅鏡が検出されている。１号鏡は面径12，6ｃｍ、2号鏡は面径11，9ｃｍ、3号鏡は面径11，5ｃｍで、いずれも倭製の変形獣形鏡あるいは四獣形鏡と呼称されるものであった。出土した黒斑のある埴輪などからこの古墳の築造時期は、津堂城山古墳と同じく、古市古墳群の形成初期と結論された。隣接する岡ミサンザイ古墳は埴輪などから5世後半または5世紀末の築造とされていることから、岡古墳は岡ミサンザイ古墳の陪塚ではなく、当古墳群内に独立した古墳として築かれたことが明らかとなっている。
==参考文献 == 天野末喜編 岡古墳－古市古墳群の調査研究調査報告1－藤井寺市文化財調査報告第5集 藤井寺市教育委員会 1989年
[[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=岡古墳 |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市藤井寺４丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|34|N|135|35|37|E|scale:20000}} |形状=方墳 |規模=全長33m |築造年代=４世紀後半 |出土品=銅鏡 船形埴輪 |特記事項=住宅建設により、ほぼ消滅 }} '''岡古墳'''（岡古墳）とは、大阪府藤井寺市藤井寺4丁目731－1に所在した[[方墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]初期の古墳であることが発掘調査で判明したが、住宅建設によりほぼ消滅している。

{{日本の古墳 |名称=岡古墳 |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市藤井寺4丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺約33m |築造年代=4世紀末頃 |出土品=銅鏡 船形埴輪 |特記事項=住宅建設によりほぼ消滅 }} '''岡古墳'''（おかこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺]]市藤井寺4丁目731-1に所在した[[方墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]初期の古墳であることが発掘調査で判明したが、住宅建設によりほぼ消滅している。
== 概要== 岡古墳は [[仲哀天皇]]陵に指定されている[[岡ミサンザイ古墳]]の周濠東側に位置する方墳であり、同古墳の[[陪塚]]ではないかと見られていた。住宅建設の予定があり、1980年（昭和55年）に大阪府教育委員会、1981年（昭和56年）には藤井寺市教育委員会によって[[発掘調査]]がなされた。調査によると、この古墳は2段に築造されており基底部で長さ34.5m、高さ5mを測る。墳丘からは黒斑のある[[円筒埴輪]]のほか家型埴輪、船形埴輪が出土している。内部構造は割竹形[[木棺]]を蔵した[[粘土槨]]の2分の1が遺存していた。[[粘土槨]]残存部は 長さ2.5m、幅1.88mであった。木棺の本体はすでに腐朽していたが、粘土槨に印された痕跡により、おおよその形状と大きさを知ることができ、外径80cmの割竹形木棺であることが明らかとなった。棺内からは3面の[[銅鏡]]が検出されている。1号鏡は面径12.6cm、2号鏡は面径11.9cm、3号鏡は面径11.5cmで、いずれも倭製の変形獣形鏡あるいは四獣形鏡と呼称されるものであった。出土した黒斑のある埴輪などからこの古墳の築造時期は、[[津堂城山古墳]]と同じく、古市古墳群の形成初期と結論された。隣接する岡ミサンザイ古墳は埴輪などから5世後半または5世紀末の築造とされていることから、岡古墳は岡ミサンザイ古墳の陪塚ではなく、当古墳群内に独立した古墳として築かれたことが明らかとなっている。
==参考文献 == 天野末喜編 岡古墳－古市古墳群の調査研究調査報告1－藤井寺市文化財調査報告第5集 藤井寺市教育委員会 1989年
{{デフォルトソート:おかこふん}} [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市]]

==築造時期== 出土した黒斑のある埴輪などからこの古墳の築造時期は、[[津堂城山古墳]]と同じく、古市古墳群の形成初期と結論された。隣接する岡ミサンザイ古墳は埴輪などから5世後半または5世紀末の築造とされていることから、岡古墳は岡ミサンザイ古墳の陪塚ではなく、当古墳群内に独立した古墳として築かれたことが明らかとなっている。
==参考文献 == * 天野末喜編『岡古墳－古市古墳群の調査研究調査報告1－藤井寺市文化財調査報告第5集』藤井寺市教育委員会、1989年
==関連項目 == *[[古市古墳群]] *[[野中古墳]]
{{デフォルトソート:おかこふん}} [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市の歴史]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=岡古墳 |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市藤井寺4丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺約33m |築造年代=4世紀末頃 |出土品=銅鏡 船形埴輪 |特記事項=住宅建設によりほぼ消滅 }} '''岡古墳'''（おかこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺4丁目731-1に所在した[[方墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]初期の古墳であることが発掘調査で判明したが、住宅建設によりほぼ消滅している。

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Japanese Burial Mound |Name=Oka Kofun |Location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in..."
revision.text:
{{Japanese Burial Mound |Name=Oka Kofun |Location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nono Kofun]]
[[Category:Square-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:岡古墳]]

{{Japanese Burial Mound |Name=Oka Kofun |Location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{Japanese Burial Mound |Name=Oka Kofun |Location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

revision.comment: redirecting the alterrnative names all to this one name
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |Type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "割竹形木棺}}" to "割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "岡ミサンザイ古墳}}" to "岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Approximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. Approximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.

[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Aqqroximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its aqqroximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:岡古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Aqqroximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. Aqqroximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its aqqroximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Aqqroximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. Aqqroximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its aqqroximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=A11roximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=A11roximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|scale:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=A11roximately 33 meters per side |Construction Period=Late 4th century |Artifacts=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== Construction Period ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==

Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm

Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate shape and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Cultural Properties Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
