revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designated as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. Artifacts such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is designation1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as deformed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early formation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is other_designation1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is other_designation1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is other_designation1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal Structure and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is other_designation1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City other_designation Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its [[Category:pp error]]approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==

Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:Square-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Oka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Oka Kofun]] [[ja:岡古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |Notable=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |destroyed=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * [[Nonaka Kofun]]

The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |destroyed=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Oka Kofun |location=Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates={{Coordinate|34|33|58|N|135|35|45|E|map_size:20000}} |type=Square-shaped tumulus (Hōfun) |area=A11roximately 33 meters per side |built=Late 4th century |burial goods=Bronze mirrors, boat-shaped haniwa |destroyed=Almost completely destroyed due to residential construction }} '''Oka Kofun''' (岡古墳) was a [[Square Kofun|square-shaped tumulus]] (Hōfun) located in [[Fujidera City]], Osaka Prefecture, specifically at 731-1 Fujidera 4-chome. Excavations revealed that it was an early kofun within the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], but residential development led to its near-total destruction.
== Overview ==
[[File:岡古墳出土 埴輪 船.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Boat-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Aisell Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center.}}]] The Oka Kofun was located east of the outer moat of {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is Historic Site1 as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was hypothesized to be a {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} (satellite mound) of this larger tumulus. Due to planned residential construction, excavations were conducted in 1980 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and in 1981 by the Fujidera City Board of Education. The surveys revealed that the kofun was built in two tiers, measuring 34.5 meters in length and 5 meters in height at its base. burial goods such as blackened {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as house-shaped and boat-shaped haniwa, were unearthed.
== Internal burial and burial goods ==
The internal structure included a {{ill|clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that housed a {{ill|split-bamboo shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. A11roximately half of the clay coffin remained intact, measuring 2.5 meters in length and 1.88 meters in width. While the wooden coffin itself had decayed, impressions left in the clay coffin allowed researchers to determine its approximate type and size, confirming it as a split-bamboo shaped coffin with an external diameter of 80 centimeters. Within the coffin, three {{ill|bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} were discovered. These mirrors, referred to as detypeed beast-shaped mirrors or quadruped mirrors, measured as follows: 1. Mirror 1: Diameter 12.6 cm 2. Mirror 2: Diameter 11.9 cm 3. Mirror 3: Diameter 11.5 cm
== built ==
Based on the blackened haniwa and other findings, the Oka Kofun was dated to the early typeation phase of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], contemporaneous with the {{ill|Tsudō Shiroyama Kofun|ja|津堂城山古墳|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adjacent {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} has been dated to the late 5th century or the end of the 5th century based on its haniwa, suggesting that the Oka Kofun was not a satellite mound of Oka Misanzai Kofun but an independent tumulus within the cluster.
== References ==
* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.

* Suezane Amano, ed. *"Oka Kofun - Investigation Report of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster Vol. 1 - Fujidera City Historic Site Survey Report No. 5"*, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1989.
